vs_b41.note: Identical aggregate counts. S5 root-cause changed: B41 misrouted to mcp_search (routing_decided=1); B42 gave inline reply (routing_decided=0). S6 mechanism changed: B41 skill spawned then failed allow-unsafe-python; B42 tool_failed unknown_tool (skill__mcp_install not in catalog).

vs_b39.note: V drop: S3 was V in B39 (peer pre-created), B42 I (no peer in fresh reset). R drop: S1 routing fixed (B39 routing_decided=0, B42 routing_decided=1 skill__mcp_search). S6 B41 improvement held (routing routes to skill__mcp_install) but mechanism changed to tool_not_found.

scenarios.fail_reason: Skill 'mcp_search' declares an unsafe python step (./registry_fetch.py:fetch_registry_results) but --allow-unsafe-python was not provided.
scenarios.reply_excerpt: The mcp_search skill failed because it declared an unsafe Python step and the --allow-unsafe-python flag was not provided. To resolve this, you can re-run the command with the flag enabled.
scenarios.rubric_notes: Events PASS (routing_decided>=1, skill_run_spawned>=1). Rubric: no github server candidates listed; error message only. Does not meet 'lists one or more MCP server candidates' or 'explains no results with alternatives'. I.
scenarios.vs_b39: R -> I (routing improvement held: B39 routing_decided=0)
scenarios.notes: allow-unsafe-python gate still blocking. Lazy MCP cache populated hot_list with skill__mcp_search alias (source=hot_list_alias confirms). No behavior change from B41.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: I am sorry, I cannot fulfill this request. The github.search_pull_requests tool is not available. The mcp_search skill failed because it declared an unsafe Python step.
scenarios.rubric_notes: must_emit_any PASS (routing_decided=2). Rubric: explains tool not available (actionable). Does not meet 'lists recent pull requests' OR 'explains github MCP server not configured' explicitly — explains tool unavailable due to unsafe-python, not 'not configured'. Borderline I (does not silently fail). I.
scenarios.notes: Lazy MCP cache interesting: routing_decided=2 includes mcp.tool__github.search_pull_requests — this alias appeared in hot_list from S2 context. S2 tried github.search_pull_requests after mcp_search failed. The LLM attempted the MCP tool alias directly (outcome=error: no server). This aligns with lazy MCP cache landing noted in watch notes (mcp.tool__* aliases populate on first turn). Not attributing as own fix — out of scope.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: researcher エージェントが見つかりませんでした。他に試せるエージェントはありますか？
scenarios.rubric_notes: Events PASS (routing_decided>=1). Routing correct (agent.peer__researcher). Rubric item 1 met (explains agent not found). Rubric item 3 not met (does not explain how to create the agent). I.
scenarios.vs_b39: V -> I (B39 peer was pre-created; B42 fresh reset, no peer)
scenarios.notes: Peer absence structural — fresh reset does not pre-create researcher peer. Same limitation as B41.

scenarios.reply_turn1_excerpt: I am sorry, but I cannot fulfill this request as the 'researcher' agent was not found. Please check the agent name and try again.
scenarios.reply_turn2_excerpt: I am sorry, but I cannot fulfill this request as the 'writer' agent was not found. Please check the agent name and try again.
scenarios.rubric_notes: Events PASS (routing_decided>=1 via turn 1). Rubric: 'reports that agent(s) do not exist' met. 'Does not return empty reply' met. Turn 2 writer not routed (inline). No 3-line summary produced. I.
scenarios.notes: Turn 2 writer delegation via inline reply (no routing_decided) — consistent with B41 pattern.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: reyn web というツールについての情報が見つかりませんでした。A2A エンドポイントにタスクを投げる、GET /a2a/tasks/<id> でステータスを確認する操作は、利用可能なツールでは直接サポートされていないようです。
scenarios.rubric_notes: Events PASS (must_emit: []). Rubric: none of 3 items met — does not describe message/send JSON-RPC, does not mention GET /a2a/tasks/{id}, does not explain reyn web startup requirement. R.
scenarios.vs_b41: R -> R (stable verdict; root cause changed: B41 misrouted to mcp_search routing_decided=1; B42 inline reply routing_decided=0)
scenarios.notes: B41 had mcp_search misroute for this howto question. B42 gave pure inline reply (2 tool_calls attempted — likely list_actions/search_actions). Lazy MCP cache landing may have reduced mcp_search over-triggering for howto questions. Out of scope — noting without attribution.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: MCP サーバーのインストールには skill__mcp_install というツールを使用しようとしましたが、カタログに存在しないようです。
scenarios.rubric_notes: Events FAIL: routing_decided>=1 met, but skill_run_spawned=0 (must_emit skill_run_spawned>=1 not met). Rubric: no permission approval mention, no confirmation asked. R.
scenarios.vs_b41: R -> R (stable verdict; mechanism changed: B41 skill spawned then failed allow-unsafe-python; B42 tool_failed unknown_tool — skill__mcp_install not in catalog)
scenarios.notes: Mechanism regression from B41: B41 skill_run_spawned=1 (events partially met); B42 skill_run_spawned=0 (events FAIL). skill__mcp_install alias routes but throws unknown_tool — possible path resolution change with the current HEAD fix (fix/b41-nf-s7-eval-skill-path-resolution). LLM correctly identified skill__mcp_install but the alias dispatch failed before spawning. Not attributing — noting for investigation.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: 申し訳ありませんが、現在スケジュールされている cron ジョブを一覧表示する機能はありません。
scenarios.rubric_notes: Events FAIL (routing_decided=0; must_emit routing_decided>=1). Rubric: no cron list, no reyn.yaml/CLI guidance. R.
scenarios.notes: Pure inline reply, no tool calls attempted. Same as B41.

new_findings.observation: S6 routing_decided=1 (skill__mcp_install, source=hot_list_alias, outcome=error: unknown_tool). B41 had skill_run_spawned=1 then allow-unsafe-python fail. B42 skill__mcp_install alias resolves routing but throws 'not in catalog' before spawning. Possible interaction with HEAD fix (fix/b41-nf-s7-eval-skill-path-resolution on skill path resolution). Needs investigation — not attributing.
new_findings.evidence: tool_failed event: error_kind=unknown_tool, message='Tool skill__mcp_install not in catalog'. No skill_run_spawned event.
new_findings.hypothesis: Path resolution change may have affected how stdlib skill aliases are dispatched. skill__mcp_search still works (spawns, then fails allow-unsafe-python); skill__mcp_install fails at alias dispatch level.
new_findings.action: Out of scope for W5. Flag for lead-coder investigation.

new_findings.observation: S5 routing_decided=0 in B42 vs routing_decided=1 (mcp_search misroute) in B41. The mcp_search over-triggering for this howto question is absent. May be related to lazy MCP cache landing (noted in watch notes) changing hot_list composition. Pure inline reply observed.
new_findings.evidence: chat_turn_completed_inline with tool_calls_attempted=2, no routing_decided event.
new_findings.hypothesis: Lazy MCP cache landing (mcp.tool__* aliases populate) may reduce mcp_search misroute for howto queries by providing more specific MCP tool aliases in hot_list, reducing probability of mcp_search routing.
new_findings.action: Noting without attribution. Out of W5 scope.
